title: bupleurum perfoliatum var. rotundifolium
intro: Bupleurum perfoliatum var. rotundifolium, also known as round-leaved hare's ear, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to its content of sesquiterpenes and saponins. The health benefits of round-leaved hare's ear include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support liver function, making it a valued herb in herbal medicine. Bupleurum perfoliatum var. rotundifolium can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
